payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: prd_review.

Intent:
Allow report-only execution leads to author narrowly-scoped report artifacts and verification receipts without weakening P11.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: PRD P1-P3 correspond to boundaries present in current source
Out-of-scope anti-goals (no global mode change, no Bash(*), no P11 semantic change, no Vela code) are honored by the diff
Receipts (pytest passed, diff-check clean) are self_reported; not re-run this session

criteria: Source symbols exist at cited lines
evidence: P1 narrow report-only policy is real and testable (allowedTools+dontAsk at command construction)
P2 normal requests untouched (empty allowed_tools, request default bypassPermissions)
P3 P11 strict semantics preserved (out-of-scope forbids P11 change; diff shows no driver P11 weakening)
confidence.rationale: All cited source boundaries verified directly this session and the PRD is internally consistent and scoped; deductions for the unverified dontA[REDACTED_API_KEY] semantic and self_reported pytest/shasum.

payload.content: prd_review ACCEPT: PRD grants a narrow command-level allowedTools policy only for explicit report-only execution requests while keeping P11 authoritative. P1-P3 map to real source boundaries (REPORT_ONLY_EXECUTION_ALLOWED_TOOLS :34 with no Bash(*), dontAsk :48, normal bypassPermissions :113, AND-gated _is_report_only_execution_request :465-494). Diff 180/1 across dual_agent_lead.py + 2 test files; no Vela product code; anti-goals honored.

assumptions_to_verify: dontAsk genuinely restricts to --allowedTools at execution time
No top-level prd.md copy diverges from source/prd.md
contradictions_checked: PRD says non-bypass mode vs impl uses dontAsk — resolved as semantic gap to verify downstream, not a PRD-quality defect
Out-of-scope forbids Bash(*) and Vela code — confirmed absent in source and diff
P11 must remain strict — diff touches no P11 deliverable-evidence semantics
missing_evidence: Behavioral proof that the Claude CLI enforces --allowedTools under dontAsk rather than auto-approving
Independent shasum confirmation of source/prd.md against pinned ba65ecc8 (approval denied)
Independent pytest re-run (approval denied; receipts self_reported)
critical_review.strongest_objection: PRD's load-bearing claim is a non-bypass permission mode making allowedTools the effective lead tool policy, but the implementation uses dontAsk and tests only assert the literal string plus tool presence/absence; if dontAsk auto-approves, the report-only path is broader than promised.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that dontAsk auto-approves tools beyond --allowedTools (would broaden the report-only path beyond PRD promise) or a source boundary diverging from the cited lines/diff would move this to revise.

decisions: P1 narrow report-only policy is real and testable (allowedTools+dontAsk at command construction)
P2 normal requests untouched (empty allowed_tools, request default bypassPermissions)
P3 P11 strict semantics preserved (out-of-scope forbids P11 change; diff shows no driver P11 weakening)
objections: dontA[REDACTED_API_KEY] of --allowedTools is asserted only as a string, not behaviorally verified (downstream verify at execution/outcome)
Detection is AND of report+deliverable markers, so report-only requests without a deliverable-marker path are conservative false-negatives

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: issues_review.

Intent:
Allow report-only execution leads to author narrowly-scoped report artifacts and verification receipts without weakening P11.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: 2 slices fully cover P1-P3 with no orphan ACs
every AC traces to a real source symbol and a non-vacuous test
P11 strictness preserved: receipt-only-without-changed-file still blocks
normal execution path unchanged (no allowedTools, bypassPermissions)
grill findings F1/F2/F3 resolved and mapped into slices

criteria: slices cover all PRD promises with no orphans
each AC backed by a real source symbol
each AC backed by a non-vacuous test
working diff matches claimed changed_files and is hygiene-clean
confidence.rationale: Source symbols and four named tests verified non-vacuous against current working tree; diff matches the git_diff receipt exactly and hygiene is clean. Held below 0.9 because pytest/shasum are self-reported in the handoff (not re-run by the lead) and the dontA[REDACTED_API_KEY] guarantee is untested downstream.

payload.content: issues_review ACCEPT: 2 slices cover all 3 PRD promises (P1,P2->Slice1; P3->Slice2) with no orphans; every AC is source+test backed in the current working diff (dual_agent_lead.py +64, two test files +42/+75; --check clean). Grill F1/F2/F3 map into slices.

assumptions_to_verify: dontAsk permission mode is non-bypass and makes allowedTools operative at lead runtime
S2-AC3 (existing deliverable-evidence tests stay green) holds beyond the focused subset — handoff P11-targeted receipt shows 7 passed
contradictions_checked: handoff git_diff receipt vs actual git diff --stat: consistent (+180/-1, 3 files)
memory line refs vs current source: minor drift only (tests :534/:810/:848), symbols all present; memory file updated
top-level issues.md is a stub but authoritative content is source/issues.md per handoff sha 501121d5
missing_evidence: runtime proof that dontAsk + --allowedTools is enforced rather than auto-approved
independent rerun of pytest and artifact shasum (self_reported in handoff)
critical_review.strongest_objection: Slice 1 AC2 asserts the permission-mode string is 'dontAsk' and that an allowedTools list is appended, but no test proves the Claude CLI actually ENFORCES that allowedTools list under dontAsk (vs auto-approving). If dontAsk auto-approves, the report-only branch is broader than AC intent.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A demonstration that dontAsk auto-approves all tools (making the narrow list cosmetic) would downgrade to revise; a failing P11 regression in the targeted suite would deny.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Allow report-only execution leads to author narrowly-scoped report artifacts and verification receipts without weakening P11.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: TDD plan tests map P1-P3 to real tested boundaries
P11 deliverable-evidence remains strict; receipt alone cannot satisfy it
report-only allowed-tools policy excludes global Bash and is execution+AND-gated

criteria: Each named test exists verbatim and is non-vacuous: PASS
Tests cover both command boundary and P11 workflow safety: PASS
Implementation symbols back the tests: PASS
dontAsk enforcement of allowedTools exercised: NOT VERIFIED (downstream CLI)
confidence.rationale: All 4 tests verified verbatim at cited lines, non-vacuous, backed by real implementation symbols; diff and diff-check verified directly by reviewer. Deductions: tests are GREEN-not-RED (RED documented but not replayed) and dontAsk CLI enforcement is a downstream assumption not exercised by unit tests; pytest receipts self-reported.

payload.content: TDD plan defines 4 non-vacuous tests mapping P1-P3 to real boundaries verified in current source: P1 report-only command gets dontAsk + narrow allowedTools with Bash(*)/Bash(rm *) absent (invoker:506), P2 normal request stays bypassPermissions with no allowedTools (invoker:534), P3 allows Vela report artifact with green P11 (driver:810) and blocks receipt-without-changed-file via accepted_gate_without_changed_files (driver:848), proving P11 unweakened. Impl REPORT_ONLY_EXECUTION_ALLOWED_TOOLS:34, dontAsk:48, _is_report_only AND-gated:465 back the tests. Grill findings 1-3 resolved. Gate should ACCEPT.

assumptions_to_verify: pytest receipts (4/23/7 passed) are accurate — self-reported, not rerun this gate
Default permission_mode for normal execution is bypassPermissions — relied on by P2, confirmed by passing test not by config read
contradictions_checked: Checked P3-block test truly uses empty gate changed_files while receipt claims a file — confirmed changed_files=[] at driver:853, so it proves receipt-alone insufficiency (non-vacuous)
Checked P1 asserts absence of Bash(*) and Bash(rm *), not just presence of safe tools — confirmed invoker:530-531
Checked _is_report_only_execution_request is AND-gated (report marker AND deliverable marker AND gate==execution) — confirmed dual_agent_lead.py:466-494, not over-broad
missing_evidence: Independent replay of RED state by reverting dual_agent_lead.py report-only branch
A direct test that a report-only instruction marker WITHOUT a deliverable marker does not trigger allowedTools (negative half of the AND-gate)
Verification that dontAsk permission mode actually restricts to allowedTools at the Claude Code CLI layer
critical_review.strongest_objection: Tests are observed GREEN, not RED; the RED-before-implementation state is documented in tdd.md but not independently replayed by this gate, so the discipline that each test actually fails without the new branch is asserted, not proven.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that any of the 4 tests passes vacuously (e.g., the AND-gate detection returns True for the P2 normal request, which would make P2's allowedTools-absent assertion meaningless), or that P11 can be satisfied by a report receipt without a real changed file, would flip this to revise/deny.

Independently review the tdd_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Allow report-only execution leads to author narrowly-scoped report artifacts and verification receipts without weakening P11.

missing_evidence: Independent replay of RED state by reverting dual_agent_lead.py report-only branch

Verification that dontAsk permission mode actually restricts to allowedTools at the Claude Code CLI layer

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Allow report-only execution leads to author narrowly-scoped report artifacts and verification receipts without weakening P11.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: Implementation plan file list matches actual src diff
Report-only narrow tool policy attaches only to explicit report-only execution requests (AND-gated)
P11 deliverable-evidence guard preserved: report receipt alone is insufficient

criteria: git file ownership matches plan: pass
named symbols exist at cited lines: pass
4 traceability tests exist verbatim and are non-vacuous: pass
P11 not weakened (negative block test asserts deliverable_evidence_failed): pass
artifact sha256 vs handoff packet: not executed (approval denied)
confidence.rationale: All plan claims verified against live source: file ownership exact, symbols real, 4 tests present and non-vacuous covering P1-P3 plus the critical negative P11 path. Confidence capped below 0.95 because pytest and shasum were not executed (approval denied) and dontAsk non-bypass enforcement is only asserted at command-construction, not at the downstream harness.

payload.content: Implementation plan is sound, complete, and traceable against current source. File ownership matches git (3 src M, +180/-1, --check clean); symbols ALLOWED_TOOLS:34 (no Bash(*)), dontAsk:48, _is_report_only AND-gated:465-494 wired :447/:454 and :442/:460; 4 traceability tests verbatim+non-vacuous map P1-P3 including negative P11 block. ACCEPT.

critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If a test showed report-only allowedTools attaching to a normal code-execution request, or if P11 accepted a report receipt with empty gate changed_files, or if a declared source/ artifact failed its handoff sha256.

critical_review.strongest_objection: P2 protection is overclaimed: a normal code-execution request whose task_id contains report-only (e.g. this task) and whose instruction references docs/dual-agent/ receives dontAsk plus narrow allowedTools. Live counterexample confirmed; the mapped P2 test uses task_id=code-change and does not refute this.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If P11 accepted a report receipt with empty changed_files, if focused or P11 pytest failed on current worktree, or if a test showed Bash(*) present in report-only allowedTools.

assumptions_to_verify: The supplied pytest receipts were produced from the current restored worktree.
Claude Code treats dontAsk plus allowedTools as non-bypass enforcement rather than broad auto-approval.
Generated top-level gate documents are not intended to be hand-authored plan artifacts.
Production report-only instructions will not falsely match ordinary code tasks that co-mention report markers and docs paths.
contradictions_checked: Claude changed_files claim vs git diff: consistent for the three modified source/test files.
Top-level exported docs differ from source docs: reconciled as generated gate logs; handoff manifest pins source/ artifacts and source hashes match.
Narrow allowedTools could leak to normal execution: checked against _is_report_only_execution_request and P2 negative test.
Report receipt could satisfy P11 without a real changed file: checked against verify_gate_deliverable_evidence and the Vela receipt-without-changed-file test.
Broad shell access risk: source and tests exclude Bash(*) and Bash(rm *).
missing_evidence: Independent pytest rerun from this read-only review session.
End-to-end proof that Claude Code enforces --permission-mode dontAsk with --allowedTools as the effective runtime boundary.
A negative test for report-only marker without a deliverable marker and deliverable marker without a report-only marker.
Path-scoping proof for Edit/MultiEdit/Write beyond prompt contract and P11 after-the-fact evidence checks.
critical_review.strongest_objection: The command-level policy is narrower than bypassPermissions but not path-scoped: Edit, MultiEdit, and Write can still write any file, and this review has no downstream proof that Claude Code enforces dontAsk plus --allowedTools exactly as intended. This is a low-severity residual risk, not a gate blocker, because the implementation plan preserves P11 and tests normal-path non-leak plus receipt-only blocking.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: I would change to revise or deny if focused pytest fails on rerun, if normal execution receives report-only allowedTools, if P11 accepts a report receipt with empty changed_files, if a source artifact hash diverges from the handoff manifest, or if Claude CLI semantics make allowedTools cosmetic under dontAsk.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Allow report-only execution leads to author narrowly-scoped report artifacts and verification receipts without weakening P11.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: Pass status is self-reported from handoff tool_receipts; in-session pytest and python import were approval-denied so I could not re-execute tests this turn.
Static diff inspection independently confirms the source and test symbols exist and are non-vacuous.

criteria: Implementation diff non-empty in ta[REDACTED_API_KEY] files (met)
P11 not weakened (met by construction + test #4)
confidence.rationale: Diff is real, non-empty, in the correct deliverable files, --check clean, and matches the accepted plan and all prior gate reviews (prd/issues/tdd/implplan ACCEPT). Source and tests inspected line-by-line confirm narrowness and P11 preservation. Below 0.95 because I could not personally re-run pytest/import (approval denied), so pass status relies on handoff receipts, and dontAsk/allowedTools are verified only at argv-construction level (CLI honoring is its own untested contract).

payload.content: Execution gate for report-only lead tool policy. Deliverable diff is present and non-empty (+180/-1 across supervisor/dual_agent_lead.py + 2 test files), git diff --check clean. Source adds narrow REPORT_ONLY_EXECUTION_ALLOWED_TOOLS (no Bash(*)), dontAsk permission mode, and AND-gated _is_report_only_execution_request wired into build_claude_lead_command. 4 non-vacuous tests prove narrowness, normal-path invariance, accept-green for a real report artifact, and block on empty changed_files. P11 deliverable-evidence gate is structurally untouched (verify_gate_deliverable_evidence not in diff). ACCEPT.

assumptions_to_verify: Handoff receipt pass statuses (4/23/7 passed) are accurate
Claude CLI applies --allowedTools and --permission-mode dontAsk as documented
contradictions_checked: Whether the allowedTools relaxation weakens P11 — it does not: P11's verify_gate_deliverable_evidence is not in the diff and test #4 proves empty changed_files still blocks even for report-only
Whether normal code-change execution is affected — it is not: test #2 confirms normal gate stays bypassPermissions with no --allowedTools
Whether the detector over-triggers — it is AND-gated requiring both a report marker and a deliverable-path marker
missing_evidence: In-session pytest run output (approval-denied)
An integration test proving the spawned CLI honors dontAsk/allowedTools at runtime
critical_review.strongest_objection: The dontAsk permission mode and narrow allowedTools are asserted only at the level of the constructed argv; whether the downstream Claude CLI actually enforces them is not verified by any in-repo test, and I could not execute pytest myself this session (approval denied), so the pass status is self-reported from handoff receipts.

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Allow report-only execution leads to author narrowly-scoped report artifacts and verification receipts without weakening P11.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: report-only leads can author narrow report artifacts
P11 deliverable evidence floor preserved
normal execution path unchanged (bypassPermissions, no allowedTools)

confidence.rationale: All static evidence verified directly against current source: diff scope, narrow allowlist with no Bash(*), dontAsk mode, AND-gated detection wired into command builder, P11 untouched, and 4 non-vacuous tests including the receipt-cannot-fake-P11 case. Confidence held below 0.95 because pytest re-run was denied by approval policy (green status self_reported) and dontAsk enforcement is verified only at argv level, not downstream CLI.

P11 (verify_gate_deliverable_evidence at dual_agent_workflow.py:414) is untouched and still blocks accepted gates whose actual changed_files are empty even when a receipt claims a report path. Diff +180/-1 across 3 files, --check clean; 4 non-vacuous tests cover both the widening and the P11 floor.
